title: Frontier AI Safety Framework Comparison Dataset
description: A non-ranking comparison of published frontier AI safety frameworks, including version history, threshold structure, risk domains, review commitments, release evidence, and public-law mappings.
citation: Vorp Labs, Frontier AI Safety Framework Comparison Dataset (data through 2026-07-12), https://vorplabs.com/ai-regulatory-updates/frontier-ai-framework-comparison
comparisonNotice: This dataset compares public documents and evidence fields. It does not score, certify, or rank organizations, and a not-verified value does not prove an internal practice is absent.

frameworks.thresholdModel: Capability thresholds paired with safeguard outcomes; current policy also uses ASL-2 and ASL-3 for maintained protection levels.
riskDomains: Chemical and biological weapons production (non-novel and novel thresholds)
Misaligned AI systems in high-stakes settings
Automated R&D in key domains (AI, energy, robotics, weapons development)
frameworks.externalReview: Risk Report review can involve one or more external reviewers; v3.4 permits section-level division when all unredacted sections are covered.
frameworks.updateCadence: Changes are proposed by the CEO and Responsible Scaling Officer and approved by the board in consultation with the Long-Term Benefit Trust; updated versions are published on or before their effective date with differences recorded in a change log.
frameworks.publicChangeHistory: Nine dated versions through v3.4, with public redlines for recent updates.
frameworks.releaseEvidence: Risk Reports and off-cycle model analyses are separate from the framework document; not every model release receives a new Risk Report.
frameworks.sb53Posture: Explicit. Anthropic publishes a separate SB 53 compliance page and the RSP states that statutory definitions are addressed in separate compliance frameworks.
frameworks.raisePosture: No public statement found as of 2026-07-04.
frameworks.euCodePosture: Full signatory of the EU GPAI Code of Practice.
frameworks.seoulPosture: Signatory; framework published before the February 2025 deadline.
frameworks.comparisonCaveat: The RSP's post-v3.0 structure does not map cleanly to its earlier numbered AI Safety Level framing.

frameworks.thresholdModel: Frontier Governance Framework Tier 1/2/3 levels map in part to Preparedness Framework High and Critical thresholds.
frameworks.externalReview: The governance framework describes external expert input; the two-document structure must be checked for the exact procedure.
frameworks.updateCadence: OpenAI commits to a Framework Assessment at least once every 12 months from the effective dates of the California TFAIA and the EU Code of Practice, with material updates documented in a changelog and published within 30 days.
frameworks.publicChangeHistory: Preparedness Framework has two public major versions; the 2026 Frontier Governance Framework is unversioned on its face.
frameworks.releaseEvidence: Preparedness findings and system cards provide model-specific evidence, while the Frontier Governance Framework is the regulatory-facing document.
frameworks.sb53Posture: Explicit. The FGF states it is OpenAI's frontier AI framework under the TFAIA, with OpenAI OpCo LLC responsible for US compliance.
frameworks.raisePosture: Not named in the FGF as of the 2026-05-28 edition.
frameworks.euCodePosture: Full signatory. The FGF doubles as the public summary of OpenAI's Safety & Security Framework under the EU Code, with OpenAI Ireland Limited the responsible EU provider.
frameworks.seoulPosture: Signatory; framework published before the February 2025 deadline.
frameworks.comparisonCaveat: OpenAI uses two governing documents with different jobs; treating either as the complete framework loses material context.

frameworks.thresholdModel: Critical Capability Levels for severe risk plus Tracked Capability Levels for earlier, significant-but-less-severe signals.
riskDomains: ML R&D acceleration/automation and misalignment (including stealth and situational awareness)
frameworks.externalReview: The framework describes holistic assessments and internal oversight; no universal independent-review requirement was verified for every evaluation.
frameworks.updateCadence: Reviewed at least once a year, and more frequently if DeepMind has reasonable grounds to believe the framework's adequacy or its adherence has been materially undermined.
frameworks.publicChangeHistory: Four dated versions from v1.0 through v3.1.
frameworks.releaseEvidence: Gemini model cards describe FSF evaluations. The Gemini 3.1 Pro card reports Deep Think below every CCL while cyber crossed an earlier alert threshold.
frameworks.sb53Posture: No explicit statement found as of 2026-07-04, despite Google plausibly meeting both statutory thresholds.
frameworks.raisePosture: No public statement found as of 2026-07-04.
frameworks.seoulPosture: Signatory (Google); framework published before the February 2025 deadline.
frameworks.comparisonCaveat: TCLs and CCLs are not direct equivalents of another lab's safeguard or preparedness tiers.

frameworks.framework: Frontier AI Framework (Advanced AI Scaling Framework v2)
frameworks.thresholdModel: Outcome-based thresholds for cyber and chemical/biological risk in the original framework; v2 detail remains only partially extracted.
frameworks.externalReview: No uniform external-review commitment was verified from the currently extracted record.
frameworks.updateCadence: No published update-cadence commitment located in the page text as of 2026-07-04.
frameworks.publicChangeHistory: Two known editions; the second is retitled Advanced AI Scaling Framework and has date ambiguity between the host page and policy index.
frameworks.releaseEvidence: No normalized release-by-release framework evidence was verified for this comparison release.
frameworks.sb53Posture: No public statement found as of 2026-07-04, despite Meta plausibly meeting both statutory thresholds.
frameworks.raisePosture: No public statement found as of 2026-07-04.
frameworks.euCodePosture: Not a signatory. Meta publicly declined to sign the EU GPAI Code of Practice.
frameworks.seoulPosture: Signatory; framework published before the February 2025 deadline.
frameworks.comparisonCaveat: The v2 PDF link is difficult to retrieve automatically, so current threshold comparisons remain incomplete.

frameworks.thresholdModel: High-risk capability categories and governance processes; the February 2026 edition adds harmful manipulation and loss of control.
frameworks.externalReview: External input is part of the governance structure, but this dataset does not claim an independent review for every covered model.
frameworks.updateCadence: The February 2026 edition adjusts the update cadence to align with regulatory obligations; the 2025 original committed to an explicit improvement discussion every six months, with updates reviewed by Microsoft's Chief Responsible AI Officer.
frameworks.publicChangeHistory: Initial February 2025 version plus a February 2026 edition with a named change log.
frameworks.releaseEvidence: The framework is governance-focused; model-specific evidence must be checked in separate Microsoft release materials.
frameworks.sb53Posture: Named. The February 2026 change log cites the CA Transparency in Frontier AI Act as a driver of the update; no explicit covered-developer statement located.
frameworks.raisePosture: Named. The February 2026 change log cites the NY RAISE Act as a driver of the update — the only tracked framework naming RAISE as of 2026-07-04.
frameworks.euCodePosture: Full signatory; the February 2026 update cites the finalized Code.
frameworks.seoulPosture: Signatory; framework published before the February 2025 deadline.
frameworks.comparisonCaveat: Month-only publication precision prevents an exact February 2026 update date.

frameworks.thresholdModel: See the source framework; normalized threshold detail is not yet complete.
frameworks.externalReview: Not verified for this comparison release.
frameworks.updateCadence: The FAIF states it will be continually adapted and updated as circumstances change, before major new capability launches, and in response to incidents.
frameworks.publicChangeHistory: Limited or not verified for this comparison release.
frameworks.releaseEvidence: Not verified for this comparison release.
frameworks.sb53Posture: Explicit. The FAIF states it complies with California's Transparency in Frontier Artificial Intelligence Act, citing Business and Professions Code sections 22757.10 et seq., and includes an AB-2013 training-data disclosure.
frameworks.raisePosture: No public statement found as of 2026-07-04.
frameworks.euCodePosture: Partial signatory: Safety and Security chapter only.
frameworks.seoulPosture: Signatory; no February 2025 framework artifact was located, making on-time Seoul delivery unconfirmed.
frameworks.comparisonCaveat: This row preserves the tracker record without inferring missing comparison fields.

frameworks.externalReview: Not verified for this comparison release.
frameworks.updateCadence: Described as a living document, revisited at least annually and updated in connection with significant technological developments.
frameworks.publicChangeHistory: Limited or not verified for this comparison release.
frameworks.releaseEvidence: Not verified for this comparison release.
frameworks.sb53Posture: No public statement found as of 2026-07-04, despite Amazon plausibly meeting both statutory thresholds.
frameworks.raisePosture: No public statement found as of 2026-07-04.
frameworks.seoulPosture: Signatory; framework published around the February 2025 deadline.

riskDomains: Enterprise harm categories including discriminatory outcomes, insecure code, CSAM, malware, prompt injection, sensitive information disclosure, and adversarial attacks

frameworks.releaseEvidence: Not verified for this comparison release.

frameworks.raisePosture: No public statement found as of 2026-07-04.

frameworks.releaseEvidence: Not verified for this comparison release.
